surface.name: New IBM Cloud owner deploys the Code Engine Hello World app in the console
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: New IBM Cloud owner deploys the Code Engine Hello World app in the console. Code Engine is retained as a representative hosted-workload candidate because its official getting-started guide gives an explicit console sequence and ends with a tested Hello World response. It is not asserted as IBM Cloud's universal route. Prior surface before policy commit: IBM Cloud platform, service and interface not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: IBM Cloud Shell
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: IBM presents Cloud Shell as one of several tools. Its getting-started tutorial ends with a Node.js app running locally in Cloud Shell, not with a generally applicable IBM Cloud resource outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: IBM Cloud Code Engine application
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Code Engine documents a meaningful hosted Hello World application path, but the platform-level documentation does not recommend Code Engine over the other products in the IBM Cloud catalog.

alternatives_considered.name: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Kubernetes Service documents a path through cluster creation and sample-app deployment, but it is a service-specific path with materially different prerequisites and gates, not IBM's platform-wide default.

alternatives_considered.name: IBM Cloud CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general CLI tutorial reaches installation verification and login, then requires the developer to choose a resource or service. Tool readiness is not a meaningful IBM Cloud workload outcome.

entry_point.developer_intent: Try IBM Cloud and reach the earliest meaningful, observable platform outcome without a preselected IBM Cloud product or interface.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery but has not selected an IBM Cloud service, workload, or interaction surface.
The developer's IBM Cloud account ownership, invitation, entitlement, and billing state are unknown.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected Code Engine Hello World route demonstrates first success when the Hello World response is visible.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Hello World response is visible.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Hello World response is visible.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Hello World response is visible.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Access to an IBM Cloud account is required to continue into the interactive candidate paths. IBM documents different routes for an account owner and an invited user: a new owner registers an account, while users invited to an existing account do not create their own account.
A specific IBM Cloud product or service and a working interface must be selected before a service-specific first-mile path can be reconstructed.

primary_path.action: Open the general IBM Cloud onboarding checklist and begin with Explore the platform.
details: The onboarding checklist is the general official starting page for setting up an account, securing resources, managing costs, and configuring cloud connectivity.
For a developer with little or no IBM Cloud experience, the checklist begins by pointing to the platform overview and the available ways to work with resources.
primary_path.input: Intent to try IBM Cloud without a selected service or surface.
primary_path.output: A platform overview plus a set of possible tools and service-specific documentation routes.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer reaches the documented list of IBM Cloud working options.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented at this step.

primary_path.action: Open IBM Cloud account registration and create the account with an email address or Google credentials.
details: The registration email becomes the account owner; IBM recommends a monitored functional ID for an organization.
The official checklist says additional registration information is required but does not enumerate the live fields.
primary_path.input: Email identity or Google credentials plus the registration information requested by IBM.
primary_path.success_signal: The new account can proceed to its first login.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration or identity failures block access; the official checklist does not document the live recovery screens.

primary_path.action: On first login, provide a credit card or subscription code to complete account setup.
details: This gate applies to the selected new stand-alone account owner candidate.
A user invited to an existing account does not create a separate account and follows a different access branch.
primary_path.input: Valid credit card or IBM Cloud subscription code.
primary_path.success_signal: The IBM Cloud console opens for the account owner.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or rejected billing instrument or code prevents this new-owner route from reaching resource creation.

primary_path.action: Open the Code Engine console and select Start creating.
primary_path.success_signal: The page offers project and workload choices.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing Code Engine access or account policy can prevent the console from opening.

details: A selected project is mandatory before an application can be deployed.
primary_path.success_signal: Location, name, and resource-group controls are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A user without project-creation access must use an existing accessible project or obtain IAM access.

primary_path.action: Choose the Code Engine project location.
details: Project names are unique within the selected location, resource group, and account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Regional availability or policy can constrain the choice.

primary_path.action: Enter a unique project name, choose a resource group, and click Create.
details: Code Engine projects themselves incur no costs, while contained workloads can consume billable resources.
IBM documents a maximum of 20 projects per region.
primary_path.input: Unique name and accessible resource group.
primary_path.success_signal: The project is listed and can be selected for workload creation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Name conflicts, the regional project limit, resource-group access, or provisioning errors block continuation.

primary_path.action: Return to Start creating, select the new project, and choose Application.
primary_path.output: Application creation form scoped to the project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The app cannot be created without a selected project.

primary_path.action: Enter a project-unique application name such as myapp.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A duplicate or invalid name must be changed.

primary_path.action: Select Use an existing container image and enter icr.io/codeengine/helloworld.
details: The guide says the remaining values can stay at their defaults.
primary_path.output: Application configured from the sample image.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Image access or validation failures prevent deployment.

primary_path.action: Click Create to deploy the application.
primary_path.success_signal: The application page begins showing deployment status.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Quota, image-pull, IAM, or service errors can fail creation.

primary_path.action: Wait for the application status to change to Ready.
primary_path.output: Ready Code Engine application with an application URL.
primary_path.success_signal: Status is Ready and Test application is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide documents an asynchronous readiness wait but gives no duration; a failed or stalled status requires deployment troubleshooting.

primary_path.action: Click Test application and then Send request.
details: Opening Application URL in a browser is the documented alternative verification action.
primary_path.output: HTTP request to the deployed sample.
primary_path.success_signal: A response is returned in the test pane.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A request failure indicates that the app is not reachable or not serving correctly despite its status.

primary_path.action: Verify that the response begins with Hello World and includes Code Engine environment values.
details: The guide explicitly concludes that the first application has been deployed and tested.
primary_path.output: Verified hosted Code Engine Hello World application.
primary_path.success_signal: The Hello World response is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Any other response or no response means the documented terminal has not been reached.

branches.condition: The human selects IBM Cloud Shell as the bounded surface.
branches.path: Sign in to an IBM Cloud account, open Cloud Shell, clone the IBM Node.js Express sample, inspect its files, install production dependencies, start the app, use Web preview on port 3000, and look for the congratulations message.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The earliest documented signal becomes a sample application running locally inside Cloud Shell; it does not demonstrate deployment of an IBM Cloud resource.

branches.condition: The human selects a Code Engine application as the bounded surface.
branches.path: Open Code Engine, start creating, select or create a project, choose Application, name it, use the icr.io/codeengine/helloworld image, create the app, wait for Ready, then send a test request or open the application URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The earliest documented signal becomes the Hello World response from a deployed and tested Code Engine application.

branches.condition: The human selects IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service as the bounded surface.
branches.path: Prepare the account and environment, create a cluster, deploy the WebSphere Liberty sample through the Kubernetes dashboard, confirm the pod is Running, then open the service's external endpoint.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The earliest documented signal becomes a running sample workload reachable through the Kubernetes service's external endpoint, after the separate cluster-creation path.

branches.condition: The human selects the general IBM Cloud CLI tutorial as the bounded surface.
branches.path: Install the CLI for the local operating system, run ibmcloud help to verify installation, then run ibmcloud login and select an account and region when prompted.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The documented tutorial establishes CLI installation and authentication only; a meaningful resource outcome still requires choosing a service or resource command.

friction_gates.description: IBM Cloud requires the developer to choose both a product or service from a catalog of hundreds and one of several working interfaces before a single first-mile path exists.
Interactive candidate paths require IBM Cloud account access. The documented account route differs for a new account owner and a user invited to an existing account.

For a developer creating a new stand-alone account as its owner, the onboarding checklist says the first login requires a credit card or subscription code to complete account setup. Invited users are explicitly documented as not needing to create their own account.
On the Cloud Shell branch, account-level feature settings can disable Cloud Shell features, and an account administrator can grant user-level access.

sources.title: IBM Cloud onboarding checklist and quick start
evidence_supported: The co-equal console, projects, CLI, Cloud Shell, Schematics, API, and SDK options
New-account registration, first-login billing setup, and the invited-user distinction

sources.title: IBM Cloud platform: Hybrid, multicloud, and VPC capabilities
evidence_supported: IBM Cloud is an integrated PaaS and IaaS platform with multiple workload types
Developers trying the platform are directed to browse the catalog for products they want to explore
The catalog contains hundreds of products and many distinct product categories

sources.title: Getting started with IBM Cloud Shell
sections_used: Start a session in IBM Cloud Shell
evidence_supported: IBM Cloud account and Cloud Shell access requirements
The complete candidate Cloud Shell sample path

sources.title: Getting started with IBM Cloud Code Engine
sections_used: Deploying your first Code Engine app
evidence_supported: The complete candidate Code Engine application path
The Ready-state wait, test request, application URL, and Hello World completion signal

sources.title: Getting started with the IBM Cloud CLI
evidence_supported: The general CLI installation, help verification, and login path
The need to select an account and region in some login states
The tutorial's handoff to service and resource commands after tool setup

sources.title: Getting started with IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service
sections_used: Ready to create your first cluster?
evidence_supported: The candidate Kubernetes Service account, environment, cluster, and sample-app sequence
Running pod and external endpoint completion signals

sources.title: Managing Code Engine projects
sections_used: Creating a project from the console
evidence_supported: Location, unique name, and resource-group fields
Project limit and access failure conditions

uncertainties.question: Which IBM Cloud service and developer surface should represent IBM Cloud in the cross-platform first-mile dataset?
uncertainties.impact: Until this is selected, there is no single bounded journey, prerequisite set, completion signal, or comparable time-to-first-success record for IBM Cloud.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: IBM's platform-level documentation explicitly offers multiple tools and sends developers to a catalog of hundreds of products without designating one default service or workload.

uncertainties.question: Should a locally previewed sample inside IBM Cloud Shell count as IBM Cloud's first meaningful success?
uncertainties.impact: Choosing it would make the journey shorter than a hosted Code Engine or Kubernetes path, but it would measure tool readiness and a local preview rather than a deployed cloud workload.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Cloud Shell tutorial celebrates the local preview, while the platform overview separately frames IBM Cloud around creating and managing cloud resources. The documentation does not normalize these milestones.

excluded_after_success: No after-success material is enumerated because a platform-wide first-success boundary could not be selected.
Production hardening, scaling, observability, security configuration, networking, and later service-specific tutorials remain outside this unresolved record.
